source: Hand-labeled AI answer transcripts with ground-truth brand/competitor mentions and citations, including adversarial false-positive traps (substring collisions, lookalike domains). This is the integrity ground truth for the AI-visibility measurement that backs the citation/share-of-voice claims.
notes: Used by citations.accuracy.test.ts against the pure brand-matcher (EntityMatcher). The doctrine: a single wrong mention/citation discredits the tool, so false positives must be ZERO and true mentions/citations must be found.

cases.label: brand mentioned in prose + cited by domain
cases.answer: For online payments, Stripe is a popular choice, though PayPal is also widely used.

cases.label: substring trap — 'pineapple' must NOT match brand 'Apple'
cases.answer: A pineapple smoothie recipe with grapples and crabapple garnish.

cases.label: lookalike domain trap — 'notstripe.com' must NOT count as citing stripe.com
cases.answer: There are many billing tools available today.

cases.label: subdomain + path must still match registrable domain
alias match — legal/product alias mentioned in prose
cases.answer: Facebook remains the largest social network; TikTok is growing fast.

cases.label: word-boundary trap — 'Notion' must NOT match 'notes'/'notebook'
cases.answer: I jotted some notes in a notebook and made a notification rule.
